branding.title: Speech synthesis demo
branding.subtitleHtml: Script name (Mia, Leo) is only a dialogue label. Voice preset picks the real intonation from a reference clip — they change line by line in the showcase.

copy.singleLineHint: Pick a voice preset and type any sentence — fastest way to test TADA.
copy.singleLineDefaultText: This is TADA on our GPU server — natural speech, cloned intonation, and reliable text alignment.
copy.dialogueCalloutHtml: Showcase scene. Mia and Leo are script names only — each line uses a different voice preset (amused host, curious character, neutral narrator…). Check the script preview, then press Run scene.

tooltips.settings_overview: These controls affect every Generate / Run on this page. Voice character (timbre, hiss) is chosen per line via Voice preset — inference sliders tune how {{engine}} generates speech.
tooltips.spoken_text: Text that {{engine}} will speak using the selected voice preset.
tooltips.generation_params: Fine-tune how {{engine}} generates audio — affects every run until you change them.
tooltips.voice_preset: Clones this reference clip's intonation and recording quality (including any mic hiss in the sample). Script names like Mia do not change the voice.
tooltips.script_name: Character name in the script only (e.g. Mia, Leo). Does not change the voice — pick Voice preset for intonation and timbre.
tooltips.tuning_lead: Voice preset = reference WAV (character + intonation). Different presets (neutral vs calm) are different clips — even for the same character (Mia). Use Stable book + one preset on all paragraphs for a uniform narrator.
